sentiment.summary: Markets in fear mode as Iran rejects peace talks, oil spikes toward $100/bbl, and the S&P posts its fifth straight losing week.

insights.text: Geopolitical meltdown drives fifth straight losing week. Iran rejected Trump's 15-point peace proposal; Trump warned of a "final blow" — oil crossed $100 intraday. VIX spiked to 29+. Energy the lone winner; everything else is bleeding. Stay cautious on speculative names.

BTC slides to $66K — 9.5% below your $73,135 cost basis. Risk-off from the Iran conflict hit crypto hard. Bitcoin is down 24.6% YTD with no macro catalyst until geopolitical tensions ease. Further downside likely if oil stays elevated.

RKLB at $62 — barely holding your $62.04 cost by a thread. Fundamentals are solid: $190M MACH-TB 2.0 contract, $2B+ backlog, 10 buy ratings. Broader risk-off is the drag, not the business. Neutron first launch targets Q4 2026 — patience required.

SATS pops to $114.96 — finally breaking even on your $114.33 cost basis. S&P 500 Equal Weighted Index addition is fueling momentum alongside SpaceX deal optimism. Fundamentals remain weak with ongoing losses, but index flows are doing the heavy lifting right now.

TE at $6.68 — just below your $6.73 cost with Q4 earnings due March 31. 2.1 GW Austin solar fab construction on track for year-end at $400-425M. Avg analyst target is $10.71. Earnings Tuesday could be a major catalyst — set a price alert.

STX at $377 — 16% below your $448.98 cost and under new AI pressure. Google's TurboQuant AI compression algo triggered a sector-wide storage selloff. Analysts still rate Buy with $385 target but AI disruption risk is real and mounting. Watch this one closely.

TSAT at ~$36 — thin cushion above your $33.33 cost after the March 17 spike faded. Jumped +19% to $42 before retreating. Multiple law firms investigating potential securities fraud is a serious red flag. Lightspeed LEO progress is the only bullish thread keeping this alive.

BBAI at $3.13 — down 65% from your $9.02 cost with no floor in sight. Stock dropped 20% this month alone. 52-week range $2.36–$9.39 shows brutal volatility. Analysts project 40% EPS improvement ahead but operational losses and weak revenue make this a speculative hold at best.

NBIS at $100 — still 24% below your $131.12 cost but catalysts are stacking up. $27B Meta AI infrastructure deal, $2B Nvidia investment, BofA Buy initiation. Avg analyst target $166.78 implies 67% upside. Conviction hold — the market simply hasn't caught up yet.

OKLO at $54 — down 57% from your $125 cost with broken support and no near-term floor. Tumbled from $194 highs despite Meta 1.2 GW Ohio partnership and DOE radioisotope facility contract. Nuclear thesis is intact long-term, but this stock needs time, not optimism.

NVTS at $8.28 — down 17% from your $10 cost and accelerating lower. Dropped 7.21% Friday on revenue miss ($45.92M below expectations) and a $31.81M net loss. GaN/SiC EV thesis is intact but execution is lagging the story. Next earnings May 7 is the line in the sand.
